title: Why Berlin Has No Beautiful Old Town - Quick Summary
items: **Berlin did have an old town:** the medieval twin settlements of Berlin and Cölln go back to the 13th century, with Cölln first documented in 1237.
**World War II devastated the centre:** by 1945, Berlin had lost huge amounts of housing and infrastructure, but the bombs did not decide every later planning choice.
**East Berlin reshaped the old centre:** the GDR demolished the palace ruins, erased much of the old Marienviertel street plan and built a socialist capital landscape.
**Nikolaiviertel is a reconstruction:** today's old-town-looking quarter was created for Berlin's 750th anniversary in 1987, not preserved unchanged from the Middle Ages.
**The missing old town is the point:** Berlin shows rupture, ideology and rebuilding more honestly than a postcard-perfect historic centre would.
